patches.hypothesis: The recursive resolution of type variables leads to a stack overflow due to infinite recursion. Adding a cycle detection mechanism using the visitedTypeVariables parameter should prevent revisiting the same type variables.
patches.changes: + Track visited type variables to prevent infinite recursion
patches.diff: + Track visited type variables to prevent infinite recursion

patches.hypothesis: The method suffers from infinite recursion due to not managing visited type variables while resolving types, causing StackOverflowError.
patches.changes: - Add logic to track visited TypeVariables to prevent infinite recursion.
patches.diff: - Add logic to track visited TypeVariables to prevent infinite recursion.

patches.hypothesis: The method is stuck in an infinite recursive loop when resolving recursive type variables due to missing mechanism to stop repeating resolutions for the same type variables.
patches.changes: Add a check to avoid resolving the same type variable multiple times which leads to an infinite loop.
patches.diff: Add a check to avoid resolving the same type variable multiple times which leads to an infinite loop.

patches.hypothesis: The resolve method causes a StackOverflowError due to infinite recursion when resolving certain recursive type variables with wildcards. This happens because visited type variables are not tracked effectively leading to redundant calls.

The method `resolve` is entering into infinite recursion due to cyclical type resolution. This is caused by recursively visiting the same type variable without checking if it has already been visited. Introducing a mechanism to track and prevent re-evaluation of already visited type variables will break the cycle.
patches.changes:
- Add a check for already visited type variables to prevent infinite recursion.
+ Modify resolve method to track visited type variables properly.
patches.diff:
- Add a check for already visited type variables to prevent infinite recursion.
+ Modify resolve method to track visited type variables properly.
